isolation.surface: CLI (Execute tool) - grep, file inspection, test execution

assertions.title: Scroll change updates only visible line runs

steps.action: Grep for test function and VAL-CORE-007 label in system/test/tests/scene.rs
steps.observed: Found at line 3527-3529: '// VAL-CORE-007: Scroll change updates only doc text runs and cursor' followed by #[test] fn incremental_scroll_updates_only_doc_and_cursor()
test incremental_scroll_updates_only_doc_and_cursor ... ok. 1 passed; 0 failed. Exit code 0.

steps.action: Read full test body (lines 3527-3675) to verify assertion coverage
steps.expected: Test builds multi-line text scene, performs scroll-only incremental update, verifies change list = [N_DOC_TEXT, N_CURSOR], verifies chrome byte-identical
steps.observed: Test creates 50 lines of text, builds full scene, then does incremental update with scroll_lines=5 (same text, different scroll_y). Asserts: change list has exactly 2 entries (N_DOC_TEXT, N_CURSOR), all 6 chrome nodes (root, title bar, title text, clock, shadow, content) are byte-identical, N_DOC_TEXT bytes differ (new text runs), cursor y=-100 (scroll-adjusted).

steps.action: Grep for scroll_runs usage in system/services/core/scene_state.rs
steps.expected: update_document_content uses scroll_runs to filter visible lines
steps.observed: Line 589: 'let visible_runs = scroll_runs(all_runs, scroll_lines, line_height, viewport_height_px);' — confirms only visible text runs are re-laid out. scroll_runs function defined at line 843 filters and repositions runs for scrolled viewport.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-CORE-007 has a dedicated test (incremental_scroll_updates_only_doc_and_cursor) that builds a 50-line text scene, performs a scroll-only incremental update, and verifies the change list contains exactly N_DOC_TEXT and N_CURSOR while all 6 chrome nodes remain byte-identical. Test passes. Implementation in scene_state.rs confirms scroll_runs filters to visible lines only.
